description: curl | sh Installer - One-line install for Seshat without Rust toolchain requirement

userStories.title: Fix release workflow to publish GitHub Releases
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want release.yml to publish GitHub Releases with prebuilt binaries when a version tag is pushed, so that install.sh can download them.
acceptanceCriteria: release.yml triggers on push: tags: ['v*'] in addition to existing push: branches: [main]
build-binaries and upload-release jobs have if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/') condition
release-plz job does not break on tag push
build-binaries generates sha256sums.txt from all built archives
upload-release attaches sha256sums.txt to GitHub Release
actionlint passes with 0 errors on all workflow files
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create install.sh with detection and download logic
userStories.description: As a developer without Rust toolchain, I want install.sh to detect my system and download Seshat, so that I can start the install process.
acceptanceCriteria: Script starts with #!/bin/sh and set -eu
OS detection with uname -s/-m maps to correct target triple (Linux/Darwin x86_64/arm64)
WSL detected when /proc/version contains microsoft
Unsupported platforms get clear error message with link to GitHub Releases
--dry-run flag shows planned actions without installing
mktemp creates temp directory, trap EXIT cleans up on exit
GitHub API fetches latest release tag_name
Downloads seshat-{target}.tar.gz and sha256sums.txt from GitHub Release
Verifies SHA256 checksum using sha256sum (Linux) or shasum -a 256 (macOS)
Extracts tarball and finds seshat binary inside
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create install.sh install logic and finalize
userStories.description: As a developer, I want install.sh to install Seshat to my system and pass all quality checks.
acceptanceCriteria: Installs binary to ~/.local/bin with mkdir -p, cp, chmod +x
Re-running is idempotent (overwrites existing binary without errors)
PATH warning displayed if ~/.local/bin is not in $PATH
Non-zero exit code on any error
POSIX sh compatible — no bash-specific syntax (#!/bin/sh)
Shellcheck: 0 warnings, 0 errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add install instructions to project docs
userStories.description: As a new user, I can find install instructions in the repository documentation.
acceptanceCriteria: tools/README.md has 'Installing Seshat' section
Section contains curl | sh one-liner with correct KSDaemon/seshat repo URL
Section contains --dry-run example
Section contains cargo install as alternative
Typecheck passes
